prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arrr, ye be askin' why the sky be blue, eh? Shore, ye look up at that vast expanse, and it be a mystery to many a landlubber! Well, gather 'round, matey, and I'll tell ye the tale of the blue sky! The light from the sun, ye see, be like a treasure chest filled with all the colors of the rainbow! Now, when that light be fightin' its way through our atmosphere, it be scatterin' about like a drunken parrot! The blue light, bein' shorter and more energetic than its brothers and sisters, be gettin' scattered every which way by the tiny particles in the air! It be like the blue gems in a treasure chest, glimmerin' and catchin' the eye more than any other color! So that's why when ye gaze upon the heavens, ye be seein' a sea of blue, me hearty! The sky be blue 'cause the blue light be the most rambunctious of the bunch, dancin' and playin' about in the air for all to see! Now, if ye be wantin' to see the sun's true colors, ye must wait 'til the golden hour, when it be settin' low on the horizon, and the atmosphere be stretchin' out the reds and oranges for ye to behold! Savvy?
